description_long: This object is a long, narrow vessel with a gently curved hull and pointed ends, resembling a canoe or kayak. The exterior is finished in a deep, glossy red, while the interior features a dark, matte surface with visible crossbars and seating slats, likely made of wood or composite material. The structure is streamlined, with a low profile and no visible blade, edge, or sharp point, and it lacks any guard or hilt. The object appears hollow, designed to accommodate one or more occupants, with open seating and support struts running across the width. There are no visible decorative elements, engravings, or ornamentation on the surface. The overall design suggests a utilitarian purpose, prioritizing stability and buoyancy over cutting or stabbing functionality.
description: A long, red, streamlined vessel with a dark interior, crossbars, and open seating, resembling a canoe or kayak. It has a glossy exterior and a hollow, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated, gently curved red hull and the open, dark interior with crossbars.
description_view_1: Top-down view revealing the pointed ends, open seating area, and internal crossbars.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, highlighting the smooth, continuous red hull.
description_view_3: Bottom-up view showing the narrow, pointed shape and the arrangement of interior supports.
attribute_handle_design: none (no handle, open seating and crossbars)
